active_mitigations.name: safe_linking
active_mitigations.description: XOR-encode FD pointers to prevent pointer forgery

bypass.method: Leak heap address, compute XOR key

active_mitigations.name: tcache_double_free_detection
active_mitigations.description: Walk tcache list to detect if chunk already present

bypass.method_1: Use different size (goes to different bin)
bypass.method_3: Overwrite tcache counts to skip check

phase_1_leak.name: Information Leak
phase_1_leak.description: Obtain base addresses for ASLR bypass
phase_1_leak.order: heap_leak typically first (needed for safe linking bypass), then libc_leak, then stack_leak

phase_2_primitive.name: Exploit Primitive
phase_2_primitive.description: Gain ability to read/write/allocate at controlled addresses

phase_3_code_execution.name: Code Execution
phase_3_code_execution.description: Redirect execution flow to gain code execution

when_to_use_fake_unsorted.condition: Cannot fill tcache OR need libc leak with limited allocations

when_to_use_tcache_fill.condition: Can allocate/free freely, no allocation limit

when_to_use_environ.condition: Have libc leak, need stack address
advantages: No need for stack canary leak

when_to_use_rop.condition: Have stack write target, CET not enforced

when_to_use_one_gadget.condition: Have stack write target, constraints satisfied
advantages: Single gadget, no ROP chain needed

when_to_use_malloc_consolidate.condition: Have scanf input, need libc leak without tcache fill

when_to_use_heap_key_leak.condition: Have UAF, need to bypass safe linking
